tying_materials: Abdomen: Pheasant tail fibers wrapped forward
Wingcase: Dark turkey tail or black foam strip
steps: Debarb hook and mount in vise. Lay a thread base from eye to bend.
Tie in 3-4 pheasant tail fibers as a short tail (half hook-gap length).
Tie in fine copper wire at the bend for ribbing.
Wrap pheasant tail fibers forward in touching turns to form a slim abdomen to the 2/3 point.
Counter-wrap copper rib in 5 even turns, tie off and trim.
Tie in a narrow strip of dark turkey tail for the wingcase.
Dub a small thorax of olive-brown hare's ear dubbing.
Pull wingcase forward over thorax, tie down behind eye, whip finish, apply head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: Dark pheasant tail fibers wrapped forward
Wingcase: Black foam or dark turkey tail
steps: Debarb hook and mount in vise. Lay dark olive thread base to bend.
Tie in 3-4 dark pheasant tail fibers as a short tail.
Tie in fine black wire at the bend.
Wrap dark pheasant tail fibers forward to the 2/3 point forming a slim abdomen.
Counter-wrap black wire rib in 5 turns, tie off.
Tie in a narrow strip of black foam for the wingcase.
Dub a compact thorax of dark hare's ear dubbing.
Pull wingcase over thorax, tie down, whip finish, head cement.

rec_blue_winged_olive_standard_emerger.hook: Tiemco TMC 2488 or curved emerger hook, sizes 18-22
tying_materials: Hook: Tiemco TMC 2488 curved emerger hook, #18-22
Shuck: Amber Zelon or brown marabou fibers
Abdomen: Olive thread or olive dubbing
Wing: Gray CDC feather or Para-post poly yarn
steps: Mount curved hook in vise. Start thread at bend.
Tie in a short trailing shuck of amber Zelon (about 1/3 hook shank).
Tie in fine copper wire ribbing at the bend.
Dub or wrap a slim olive abdomen to the 60% point.
Rib with copper wire in 4-5 open turns, tie off.
Tie in a single gray CDC feather upright as the emerging wing.
Dub a thorax of olive hare's ear around the wing base.
Whip finish and apply head cement to thread head.

tying_materials: Shuck: Dark brown Zelon or marabou
steps: Mount curved hook. Lay dark olive thread base to the bend.
Tie in a short dark brown Zelon shuck trailing behind the hook bend.
Tie in fine black wire for ribbing.
Build a slim dark olive abdomen to the 60% mark.
Counter-rib with black wire in 4-5 turns.
Tie in a dark gray CDC feather upright.
Dub dark hare's ear thorax around CDC base.
Whip finish, trim CDC to wing length, head cement.

rec_blue_winged_olive_standard_dun.hook: Tiemco TMC 100 or dry fly hook, sizes 18-22
tying_materials: Body: Olive dubbing or stripped peacock quill
Wing: Gray poly wing or CDC upwings
Hackle: Blue dun dry fly hackle
steps: Mount dry fly hook. Start thread behind eye and tie in gray poly wing upright.
Figure-eight thread to divide wing into two upright sections.
Wrap thread to bend; tie in 6-8 blue dun hackle fibers, split with figure-eight to form a cocked tail.
Dub a slim tapered olive body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in a blue dun hackle feather behind the wing.
Wrap hackle 3-4 turns behind and 2 turns in front of wing, tie off.
Build a small neat head, whip finish, apply head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Dark blue dun hackle fibers (split)
Wing: Dark gray CDC or poly wing
steps: Mount hook. Tie in dark gray poly wing upright, figure-eight to divide.
Wrap to bend. Tie in split tail of dark blue dun hackle fibers.
Dub a slim dark olive body forward to behind the wing.
Tie in dark blue dun hackle feather.
Wrap hackle 3-4 turns behind wing, 1-2 in front, tie off.
Build neat thread head, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Blue dun hackle fibers (split, long)
Body: Rusty olive dubbing or brown thread
Wing: Clear or white poly yarn (spent, figure-eight)
steps: Mount hook. At mid-shank, tie in a length of white poly yarn and figure-eight to form spent wings.
Wrap thread to bend; tie in 6-8 blue dun fibers as a long split tail.
Dub a slim rusty olive body from bend to just behind wings.
Dub a small dark olive thorax figure-eighting around the wing tie-in point.
Build a neat head, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Dark dun hackle fibers (split, long)
Body: Dark brown dubbing or thread
steps: Mount hook. Figure-eight clear poly yarn spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split tail of dark dun hackle fibers.
Dub a dark brown body from bend to behind wings.
Dub a black thorax, figure-eighting around wing roots.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Legs: Partridge fibers or brown hen back
steps: Mount hook, lay brown thread base to bend.
Tie in 4-5 pheasant tail fibers as tail, one hook-gap length.
Tie in fine gold wire for ribbing.
Dub a tapered brown hare's ear abdomen to the 60% mark.
Rib in 5 open turns with gold wire, tie off.
Tie in a dark turkey tail wingcase strip.
Tie in partridge fiber legs on each side.
Dub a round brown hare's ear thorax.
Pull wingcase forward, tie down, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Body: Pinkish-tan Fox fur dubbing or Hendrickson pink dubbing
Wing: Lemon wood duck flank or gray poly wing
Hackle: Blue dun dry fly hackle
steps: Mount hook. Tie in lemon wood duck wing fibers upright, figure-eight to divide.
Wrap to bend; tie in a split tail of blue dun hackle fibers.
Dub a pinkish-tan fox fur body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in blue dun hackle feather, wrap 4-5 turns.
Tie off hackle, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Blue dun hackle fibers (long split)
steps: Mount hook. Tie in white poly yarn spent wings at mid-shank, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split tail.
Dub a rusty brown body from bend to just behind wings.
Dub a thorax figure-eighting around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: Tan-olive hare's ear or Sulfur dubbing
steps: Mount hook, lay tan thread base to bend.
Tie in 3-4 pheasant tail fibers as tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a slim tan-olive abdomen to the 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire in 5 turns.
Tie in turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in light partridge legs on each side.
Dub tan-yellow thorax, pull wingcase forward, whip finish.
Mount hook, lay yellow thread base to bend.
Tie in cream pheasant tail fibers as a short tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a bright yellow-cream abdomen to the 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire.
Tie in light turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in tan partridge legs.
Dub bright yellow thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Gray or tan CDC feather
steps: Mount curved hook, start thread at bend.
Tie in a short amber Zelon shuck.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub slim tan-orange abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 4 open turns.
Tie in CDC feather upright as emerging wing.
Dub orange-tan thorax around wing base, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: White or pale gray CDC
steps: Mount curved hook. Tie in pale cream Zelon shuck at bend.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub bright yellow abdomen to 60% mark.
Rib forward in 4 turns.
Tie in pale gray CDC upright.
Dub bright yellow thorax, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Wing: Lemon wood duck flank or pale gray CDC
Hackle: Cream or pale ginger hackle
steps: Mount hook. Tie in wing material upright, figure-eight to divide.
Wrap to bend; tie in split cream hackle fiber tail.
Dub a pale orange-yellow body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in cream hackle feather, wrap 4-5 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Wing: White poly or pale CDC
steps: Mount hook. Tie in white poly wing upright.
Wrap to bend; tie in cream split tail.
Dub bright yellow body forward to behind wing.
Wrap pale ginger hackle 4-5 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split cream tail.
Dub an orange-rust body from bend to behind wings.
Dub orange thorax around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings.
Wrap to bend; tie in long cream split tail.
Dub bright yellow body to behind wings.
Dub yellow thorax, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Gills: Gray ostrich herl (tied along sides)
steps: Mount 3XL hook. Build thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 pheasant tail fibers spread as tails.
Tie in gold wire ribbing.
Dub a fat olive-cream abdomen to 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire in 6 turns.
Tie in ostrich herl gills along each side of thorax area.
Tie in wide mottled turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in partridge legs, dub full olive-brown thorax.
Pull wingcase over, tie down, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Moose body hair or dark elk hair (split)
Body: Olive-cream dubbing with yellow segments
Rib: Yellow thread or fine gold wire
Wing: Dyed green elk hair or deer hair comparadun style
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in a comparadun-style wing of green elk hair, fanned 180 degrees.
Wrap to bend; tie in dark moose hair split tail.
Dub a fat olive-cream body to behind wing, segmented with yellow thread rib.
Tie in grizzly-olive hackle, wrap 4 turns behind wing and 2 in front.
Tie off hackle, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Moose body hair (3, long split)
Wing: White poly yarn spent wings
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in white poly spent wings at mid-shank, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in 3 moose hair fibers as long spread tails.
Dub olive-brown body from bend to behind wings.
Dub dark olive thorax around wing roots.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: Light tan hare's ear or cream dubbing
steps: Mount hook. Lay tan thread base to bend.
Tie in wood duck flank fibers as a short tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a light tan abdomen to 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire in 5 turns.
Tie in light turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in wood duck flank leg fibers on each side.
Dub cream thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Body: Cream fox fur or light dubbing
Wing: Lemon wood duck flank fibers
steps: Mount hook. Tie in lemon wood duck wing upright, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in cream ginger hackle split tail.
Dub cream fox fur body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in cream ginger hackle, wrap 4-5 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split cream tail.
Dub cream body from bend to behind wings.
Dub cream thorax around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Lay pale yellow thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 pheasant tail fibers as tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub pale olive-tan abdomen to 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire in 5 turns.
Tie in turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in light partridge legs.
Dub pale yellow-olive thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount hook. Lay cream thread base to bend.
Tie in cream pheasant tail fibers as tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub pale cream abdomen to 60%.
Rib with gold wire.
Tie in pale turkey tail wingcase and light partridge legs.
Dub cream thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Tie in amber Zelon shuck at bend.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub pale olive abdomen to 60%.
Rib with wire in 4 turns.
Tie in gray CDC wing upright.
Dub yellow-olive thorax around CDC base, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Tie in pale Zelon shuck at bend.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub cream abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in white CDC upright.
Dub cream thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Tail: Cream or pale ginger hackle fibers (split)
Wing: Gray poly wing or pale gray CDC
Hackle: Cream or pale ginger hackle
steps: Mount hook. Tie in pale gray wing upright, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in split cream hackle tail.
Dub pale yellow-olive body from bend to behind wing.
Wrap pale ginger hackle 4-5 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Wing: White poly wing or white CDC
steps: Mount hook. Tie in white poly wing upright.
Wrap to bend; tie in white split hackle tail.
Dub cream body from bend to behind wing.
Wrap cream hackle 4-5 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split cream tail.
Dub rusty-orange body to behind wings.
Dub rusty thorax around wing roots.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings.
Wrap to bend; tie in long cream split tail.
Dub pale cream-yellow body to behind wings.
Dub pale yellow thorax, whip finish.

rec_western_trico_standard_nymph.thread: Uni 17/0 or UTC 70 Black or Olive
tying_materials: Wingcase: Black foam or turkey biot
steps: Mount tiny hook in vise. Use fine thread only.
Tie in 3 white hackle fibers as tail.
Build a slim olive-black thread abdomen to the 60% mark.
Counter-rib with fine wire if desired.
Tie in tiny black foam or biot wingcase strip.
Dub a micro-thorax of black dubbing.
Pull wingcase, whip finish with several half-hitches, cement head.

tying_materials: Wing: White poly or CDC upright
Hackle: White or pale dun (2 turns only)
steps: Mount tiny hook. Tie in micro poly wing upright.
Wrap to bend; tie in 3 white fibers as short split tail.
Build an olive-white thread body to behind wing.
Tie in white hackle, take 2 careful turns, tie off.
Whip finish with several half-hitches, head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: White thread or white poly body
steps: Mount tiny hook. Figure-eight white poly micro-wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in 3 long white split tails.
Build a white thread abdomen forward to just behind wings.
Dub a small black thorax figure-eighting around wing roots.
Whip finish, multiple half-hitches, head cement.
Mount hook. Lay brown thread base to bend.
Tie in 3-4 pheasant tail fibers as tail.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub fat brown hare's ear abdomen to 60%.
Rib with copper wire in 5 turns.
Tie in mottled turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in brown partridge legs.
Dub brown hare's ear thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Tie in brown Zelon shuck at bend.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub brown abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in brown CDC wing upright.
Dub brown hare's ear thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Mottled brown turkey quill or comparadun deer hair
steps: Mount hook. Tie in comparadun-style deer hair wing fanned 180 degrees.
Wrap to bend; tie in split brown hackle tail.
Dub tan-brown hare's ear body from bend to behind wing.
Rib with gold wire.
Wrap mixed brown-grizzly hackle 4-5 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in wood duck flank as a short tail.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub olive-gray hare's ear abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in dark turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in brown partridge legs.
Dub gray hare's ear thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Wood duck flank fibers upright
steps: Mount hook. Tie in wood duck flank wing upright, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in split blue dun hackle tail.
Tie in a stripped peacock quill at the bend.
Wrap quill forward in touching turns forming a segmented body to behind wing.
Tie off quill, tie in blue dun hackle, wrap 4-5 turns.
Whip finish, apply head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Blue dun hackle fibers (long split)
steps: Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long blue dun split tail.
Dub gray body from bend to behind wings.
Dub gray thorax around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Gills: White or cream ostrich herl (sides)
steps: Mount hook. Lay dark brown thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 long pheasant tail fibers as tail.
Tie in fine copper wire.
Dub a fat dark mahogany-brown abdomen to 60%.
Rib with copper wire in 5 turns.
Tie in white ostrich herl on each side as gills.
Tie in dark mottled turkey tail wingcase and brown partridge legs.
Dub dark brown thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Dark gray deer hair (comparadun) or dark poly wing
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in dark gray comparadun deer hair wing.
Wrap to bend; tie in split dark dun hackle tail.
Dub dark mahogany body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in dark blue dun hackle, wrap 4-5 turns.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Dark dun hackle fibers (long split)
steps: Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long dark dun split tail.
Dub dark mahogany body from bend to behind wings.
Dub dark brown thorax around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: Tan caddis dubbing or hare's ear
steps: Optional: slide bead on hook before mounting. Mount hook.
Lay tan thread base from bead to bend.
Tie in fine gold wire at bend.
Dub slim tan abdomen to 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire in 5 turns.
Tie in mottled turkey tail wingcase and tan partridge legs.
Dub brown hare's ear thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount hook. Lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in fine copper wire.
Dub olive abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in dark turkey wingcase and olive partridge legs.
Dub olive-brown thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount hook. Lay black thread base to bend.
Tie in fine black wire.
Dub black abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in black wingcase and black hen back legs.
Dub black thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Shuck: Amber Zelon or brown yarn
Wing: Tan deer hair or tan CDC
steps: Mount curved hook. Tie in amber Zelon shuck at bend.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub tan abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in tan deer hair or CDC as emerging wing, slanted back 45 degrees.
Dub tan thorax, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Tie in olive Zelon shuck.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub olive abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in olive CDC wing slanted back.
Dub olive thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Dark gray CDC or deer hair
steps: Mount curved hook. Tie in dark brown Zelon shuck.
Tie in black wire.
Dub black abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in dark gray CDC wing slanted back.
Dub black thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Body: Tan dubbing or hare's ear
steps: Mount hook. Lay thread base to bend.
Tie in a brown saddle hackle feather at the bend tip-first.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a slim tan body from bend to 3/4 shank.
Palmer hackle forward to the 3/4 mark in 5 open spirals.
Counter-rib gold wire to lock hackle fibers, tie off.
Tie in a generous bunch of natural tan elk hair as roof-style wing extending to hook bend.
Trim elk butts cleanly, build neat head, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Wing: Bleached or natural elk hair
steps: Mount hook. Tie in brown saddle hackle at bend.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub olive body from bend to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open turns.
Counter-rib copper wire, tie off.
Tie in elk hair wing, trim butts, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Dark elk hair or moose body hair
steps: Mount hook. Tie in black saddle hackle at bend.
Tie in black wire.
Dub black body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open turns.
Counter-rib wire, tie off.
Tie in dark elk/moose wing, trim butts, whip finish.
Mount hook. Lay brown thread base to bend.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub brown-olive abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in dark turkey wingcase and partridge legs.
Dub darker thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount hook. Lay dark brown thread base to bend.
Tie in black wire.
Dub dark brown abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in black foam wingcase and dark partridge legs.
Dub black-brown thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Tie in brown Zelon shuck.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub olive-brown abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in brown CDC wing slanted back.
Dub brown thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Dark CDC or deer hair
steps: Mount curved hook. Tie in dark Zelon shuck.
Tie in black wire.
Dub dark brown abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in dark CDC wing.
Dub dark thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Natural deer hair or tan elk hair
Head: Green egg sac (bright green dubbing ball, optional)
steps: Mount hook. Tie in brown hackle at bend.
Dub olive-brown body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open spirals.
Tie in deer/elk hair wing.
Trim butts. Optional: tie bright green dubbing ball at the front as egg sac.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Wing: Dark deer hair or dark elk hair
steps: Mount hook. Tie in dark brown hackle at bend.
Dub dark body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 turns.
Tie in dark deer hair wing, trim butts.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Legs: Brown rubber legs or partridge
steps: Mount 3XL hook. Lay orange thread base to bend.
Tie in medium copper wire.
Dub a fat orange-brown abdomen to 60%.
Rib with copper wire in 5 turns.
Tie in wide dark turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in brown rubber legs or partridge.
Dub round orange-brown thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Shuck: Brown marabou or burnt orange Zelon
steps: Mount larger curved hook. Tie in burnt orange Zelon shuck.
Tie in copper wire.
Dub fat orange abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in a full orange CDC wing.
Dub orange-brown thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Orange-dyed elk hair or coastal deer hair
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in large brown saddle hackle at bend.
Dub a full burnt orange body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open spirals.
Tie in a large bunch of orange elk hair wing.
Trim butts, build a large neat head, whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Lay tan thread base to bend.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub cinnamon-tan abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in light turkey wingcase and partridge legs.
Dub cinnamon thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Tie in tan Zelon shuck.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub cinnamon abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 4 turns.
Tie in tan CDC wing.
Dub cinnamon thorax, whip finish.
Mount hook. Tie in ginger saddle hackle at bend.
Dub cinnamon body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 turns.
Tie in light tan elk hair wing.
Trim butts, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: 2 black rubber legs or goose biots
Abdomen: Dark brown-black dubbing or yarn
Antennae: Black rubber or goose biot
steps: Mount large 3XL hook. Lay heavy black thread base.
Tie in 2 black rubber legs or biots as tail.
Tie in heavy copper wire for ribbing.
Dub or wrap a fat dark brown-black abdomen to 60%.
Rib with copper wire in 5-6 turns.
Tie in dark ostrich herl on sides as gills.
Tie in multi-section black foam wingcase.
Tie in black rubber legs on each side.
Dub a heavy round dark brown thorax.
Pull foam wingcase over in sections, tie down. Tie in antennae, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Natural dark elk hair or deer hair
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in a short elk hair tail at bend.
Tie in large brown saddle hackle.
Dub a thick orange-black body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open spirals.
Tie in black rubber legs on each side.
Tie in a large bunch of dark elk hair as wing.
Trim elk butts, build a large head, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: Golden-tan dubbing or biot body
Legs: Brown partridge or rubber legs
steps: Mount 3XL hook. Lay golden thread base.
Tie in 2 brown goose biots as split tails.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub or wrap a fat golden-tan abdomen to 60%.
Rib with gold wire in 5-6 turns.
Tie in mottled turkey wingcase.
Tie in partridge or rubber legs on each side.
Dub amber thorax, pull wingcase, tie in biot antennae, whip finish.

tying_materials: Hi-Vis Post: Orange or yellow foam indicator
steps: Mount 3XL hook. Lay golden thread base.
Tie in yellow biots as split tails.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub a bright gold abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5-6 turns.
Tie in tan foam wingcase and yellow rubber legs.
Dub bright amber thorax.
Pull foam over thorax; tie in a small orange foam indicator on top.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount 2XL hook. Tie in short elk tail.
Tie in brown saddle hackle and gold wire.
Dub golden yellow body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open spirals.
Counter-rib gold wire.
Tie in elk hair wing, trim butts, whip finish.

tying_materials: Hi-Vis Post: Orange poly yarn or foam indicator
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in elk tail.
Dub bright gold body to 3/4; palmer hackle.
Tie in elk wing.
At the front, tie in an orange poly yarn indicator post upright.
Trim butts, whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Lay yellow thread base.
Tie in 2 yellow biots as split tails.
Tie in gold wire.
Dub yellow abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in yellow-tan turkey wingcase and pale partridge legs.
Dub yellow thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Pale yellow elk or deer hair
steps: Mount hook. Tie in short cream tail.
Tie in pale ginger hackle at bend.
Dub yellow body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 open spirals.
Tie in pale yellow elk hair wing.
Trim butts, whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Lay black thread base.
Tie in 2 short black biots as tails.
Tie in fine black wire.
Dub slim black abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in black foam wingcase and hen back legs.
Dub black thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: Dark gray or black elk hair
steps: Mount hook. Tie in black saddle hackle at bend.
Dub black body to 3/4.
Palmer hackle in 5 spirals.
Tie in dark gray elk hair wing.
Trim butts, whip finish, head cement.

rec_zebra_midge_red_nymph.hook: Tiemco TMC 2487 or curved hook, sizes 18-22
steps: Slide silver tungsten bead onto hook. Mount in vise.
Lay red thread base from bead to bend.
Tie in very fine silver wire at the bend.
Wrap red thread forward in tight touching turns to the bead — this is the body.
Counter-wrap silver wire forward in 6-8 even segments.
Tie off wire, dub a tiny sparse black thorax behind the bead.
Whip finish behind bead, apply head cement or UV resin.

tying_materials: Bead: Small silver or gold bead
steps: Slide bead on hook, mount in vise.
Lay black thread base to bend.
Tie in fine silver wire.
Wrap black thread body forward to bead.
Counter-wrap silver wire in 6-8 segments.
Tie off wire, dub tiny black thorax.
Whip finish, UV resin or head cement.
Slide copper bead, mount hook.
Lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Wrap olive thread body to bead.
Counter-wrap gold wire in 6-8 segments.
Dub tiny dark olive thorax, whip finish.
Slide gold bead, mount hook.
Lay cream thread base to bend.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Wrap cream thread body to bead.
Counter-wrap gold wire in 6-8 segments.
Dub tiny cream thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Wing: White or gray CDC puff (small)
steps: Mount curved hook. Lay red thread base to bend.
Tie in fine silver wire.
Wrap red thread body to 70%.
Counter-wrap silver wire in 6 turns.
Tie in a tiny CDC puff as emerging wing.
Dub sparse black thorax, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Lay black thread base to bend.
Tie in fine silver wire.
Wrap black thread body to 70%.
Counter-wrap wire in 6 turns.
Tie in white CDC puff.
Dub black thorax, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Lay olive thread base.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Wrap olive thread body to 70%.
Counter-wrap wire in 6 turns.
Tie in gray CDC puff.
Dub dark olive thorax, whip finish.
Mount curved hook. Lay cream thread base.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Wrap cream thread body to 70%.
Counter-wrap wire in 6 turns.
Tie in white CDC puff.
Dub cream thorax, whip finish.

tying_materials: Bead: Small red or silver bead
steps: Slide red bead, mount hook.
Lay red thread base to bend.
Tie in silver wire.
Wrap red thread body forward; coat with UV resin or thin varnish for shine.
Counter-wrap silver wire in 7-8 tight segments.
Dub small black thorax behind bead.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Wing: White CDC puff or white poly yarn
steps: Mount curved hook. Lay red thread base to bend.
Tie in fine silver wire.
Wrap red thread body to 70%.
Counter-wrap silver wire in 7 segments.
Tie in white CDC puff or white poly as wing indicator.
Dub black thorax, whip finish.

rec_san_juan_worm_red_nymph.hook: Tiemco TMC 2457 or scud hook, sizes 12-14
tying_materials: Body: Red Ultra Chenille (Micro or Standard)
Worm ends: Singed with lighter for taper
steps: Mount scud hook in vise. Start red thread at mid-shank.
Cut a 2-inch piece of red Ultra Chenille.
Singe both ends lightly with a lighter to create a tapered, rounded tip — don't burn too much.
Tie the chenille down at the mid-point of the hook, leaving equal tails on each side.
Wrap thread over the chenille toward the bend, then back to the eye, securing it firmly.
Build a neat thread head, whip finish, apply a drop of UV resin or head cement.

tying_materials: Body: Pink Ultra Chenille (Micro or Standard)
steps: Mount scud hook. Start pink thread at mid-shank.
Cut 2-inch piece of pink Ultra Chenille. Singe both ends.
Tie chenille at mid-point, equal tails hanging each side.
Wrap thread to secure, build head, whip finish, UV resin.

tying_materials: Worm ends: Leave free to wiggle
steps: Mount scud hook. Start orange thread at mid-shank.
Cut a 2-inch length of orange Squirmy Wormy silicone material.
Tie at mid-point, leaving equal tails.
Make several firm thread wraps over the center to anchor.
Do NOT singe Squirmy material — leave ends free for movement.
Build thread head, whip finish, UV resin.
Mount hook. Lay black thread base to bend.
Tie in 2 brown goose biots forked at an angle as tail.
Tie in fine gold oval tinsel for ribbing.
Tie in 3-4 peacock herl strands; twist with thread to form a rope, wrap forward to 3/4.
Rib with gold tinsel in 5 open turns, tie off.
Tie in a soft brown hen neck hackle; make 2-3 turns, tie off.
Tie in 2 white goose biots flat over the back, angled out and crossed.
Build neat head, whip finish, head cement.
Slide gold bead onto hook, mount in vise.
Lay black thread base from bead to bend.
Tie in 2 brown biots as forked tail.
Tie in gold oval tinsel.
Tie in 3-4 peacock herl, twist into rope, wrap to bead.
Rib with gold tinsel in 5 turns.
Wrap 2-3 turns of brown hen hackle.
Tie in 2 white biots flat over the back.
Whip finish behind bead, head cement.

tying_materials: Abdomen: Black dubbing (large round ball, rear)
Legs: Black hackle (2-3 turns) or knotted black monofilament
Wing (optional): White poly or CDC for visibility
steps: Mount hook. Build a thread base.
At the rear 2/3 of the hook, dub a large round ball of black dubbing — this is the abdomen.
Wrap thread forward through a narrow waist section.
Dub a smaller ball of black dubbing at the front 1/3 — this is the thorax.
Tie in a black hackle feather at the waist; make 2-3 turns between the two dubbing balls.
Optional: tie in a small white poly wing indicator flat over the waist.
Build a neat head, whip finish, apply head cement.

tying_materials: Legs: Brown or ginger hackle (2-3 turns)
steps: Mount hook. Dub a large cinnamon-brown ball at the rear 2/3 for abdomen.
Wrap a narrow waist of brown thread.
Dub a smaller cinnamon thorax at front 1/3.
Wrap 2-3 turns of brown/ginger hackle between abdomen and thorax.
Optional: add white poly wing indicator.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Short red yarn or elk hair
Body: Tan or yellow deer body hair (spun and trimmed) or foam
Wing: Oak mottled turkey quill or tan elk hair
Legs: Knotted pheasant tail fibers or rubber legs (yellow)
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in a short red yarn tail.
Spin a large clump of tan deer hair at the bend; pack tight.
Continue spinning and packing deer hair along 2/3 of the shank for the body.
Trim body to a flat bottom, rounded top, roughly cylindrical hopper shape.
Tie in mottled turkey wing quill flat over the body, extending to hook bend.
Tie in knotted pheasant tail or yellow rubber legs on each side.
Spin deer hair for a large head, trim to shape.
Whip finish, apply head cement to thread.

tying_materials: Body: Yellow closed-cell foam (shaped) or yellow deer hair
Rib: Brown thread or tying thread
Wing: Yellow elk hair or mottled turkey quill
Head: Yellow foam or deer hair
steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in red yarn tail.
Cut a strip of yellow closed-cell foam and tie in at bend.
Wrap thread in touching turns to 3/4; fold foam over and segment with thread wraps.
Tie in mottled turkey or yellow elk wing flat over body.
Tie in yellow rubber legs on each side.
Tie in a yellow foam head; tie off with a whip finish.
Trim head to shape, apply head cement.

tying_materials: Body/Underbody: Peacock herl or black dubbing
Legs: Black rubber legs or black hackle
Indicator: Orange or red foam dot (optional)
steps: Mount hook. Tie in a strip of black foam at the bend, leaving a tag behind for the shell.
Tie in peacock herl or dub a black body forward to 3/4 of hook.
If using rubber legs, tie in 3 on each side.
Pull black foam over the body and tie down firmly at 3/4 mark.
Leave a small foam tab sticking out behind for a tail-like shell effect.
Optional: glue a small orange foam dot on top for visibility.
Whip finish, head cement.

rec_woolly_bugger_black_streamer.hook: Tiemco TMC 5263 3XL or Streamer Hook, sizes 4-10
tying_materials: Bead (optional): Large black tungsten or cone head
Rib: Fine copper or silver wire
steps: Optional: slide bead/cone on hook before mounting.
Mount hook, lay thread base from bead/eye to bend.
Tie in a generous clump of black marabou as tail — equal to hook length.
Tie in fine copper wire for ribbing.
Tie in a black saddle hackle feather at the bend (tip-first).
Tie in black chenille at bend.
Wrap chenille forward in touching turns to the bead, tie off.
Palmer hackle forward over the chenille in 5-6 even spirals, tie off.
Counter-rib copper wire to lock hackle, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials: Bead (optional): Copper or olive tungsten bead
steps: Optional: slide bead on hook.
Mount hook, lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in olive marabou tail, hook-shank length.
Tie in copper wire.
Tie in olive saddle hackle tip-first.
Tie in olive chenille; wrap forward to bead.
Palmer hackle forward in 5-6 spirals.
Counter-rib copper wire, whip finish.
Optional: slide silver bead.
Mount hook, lay white thread base to bend.
Tie in white marabou tail.
Tie in fine silver wire.
Tie in white saddle hackle tip-first.
Tie in white chenille; wrap forward to bead.
Palmer hackle in 5-6 spirals.
Counter-rib silver wire, whip finish.

tying_materials: Tail: Olive marabou (long) + a few olive Krystal Flash strands
Body: Olive schlappen or marabou wrapped forward
Pectoral fins: Mottled olive hen saddle or grouse (tied flat)
Head: Olive deer hair (spun and trimmed flat/sculpin-shaped) or large olive conehead
steps: Mount hook. Lay olive thread base.
Tie in a long, full tail of olive marabou plus 4-6 strands Krystal Flash.
Tie in medium copper wire at bend.
Tie in olive schlappen or marabou feather at bend.
Wrap feather forward in touching turns building a full body, tie off at 3/4.
Rib with copper wire in 5 turns.
Tie in flat mottled hen saddle pectoral fins on each side.
Spin olive deer hair for a sculpin-shaped head and trim flat on bottom.
Whip finish, coat head thread with head cement.

tying_materials: Tail: Natural brown marabou (long) + copper Krystal Flash
Body: Natural brown schlappen or marabou
Pectoral fins: Mottled brown grouse or hen saddle
Head: Natural deer hair (spun, sculpin-trimmed) or natural brown conehead
steps: Mount hook. Lay brown thread base.
Tie in long natural brown marabou tail plus copper Krystal Flash.
Tie in copper wire at bend.
Tie in brown schlappen feather; wrap forward in touching turns to 3/4.
Rib with copper wire in 5 turns.
Tie in mottled grouse or brown hen pectoral fins flat on each side.
Spin natural deer hair for head; pack and trim to flat-bottom sculpin profile.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Abdomen
tying_materials.description: Dark mahogany-brown dubbing or pheasant tail fibers

steps: Mount hook. Lay dark brown thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 dark pheasant tail fibers as tail, hook-gap length.
Tie in fine copper wire for ribbing.
Wrap pheasant tail fibers or dub dark mahogany abdomen to 60% mark.
Counter-wrap copper wire in 5 turns, tie off.
Tie in dark turkey tail wingcase strip.
Tie in dark partridge leg fibers on each side.
Dub a compact dark mahogany thorax.
Pull wingcase forward, tie down, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: Dark blue dun hackle fibers, split

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: Dark gray CDC or comparadun deer hair

tying_materials.name: Hackle
tying_materials.description: Dark blue dun or brown dry fly hackle

steps: Mount hook. Tie in dark gray CDC or comparadun deer hair wing upright.
Figure-eight thread to secure wing.
Wrap thread to bend; tie in split dark dun hackle fiber tail.
Dub a slim dark mahogany body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in dark dun hackle; wrap 4 turns behind wing and 1-2 in front.
Tie off hackle, build neat head, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: Dark dun hackle fibers, long split

steps: Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly yarn spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split dark dun tail.
Dub dark mahogany-rust body from bend to behind wings.
Dub dark brown thorax figure-eighting around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: 3 speckled wood duck or mallard flank fibers

tying_materials.name: Legs
tying_materials.description: Speckled partridge or wood duck flank

steps: Mount hook. Lay olive-brown thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 speckled wood duck fibers as tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a slim gray-olive abdomen to 60% mark.
Rib with gold wire in 5 turns.
Tie in mottled turkey tail wingcase and speckled partridge legs.
Dub olive-gray thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Shuck
tying_materials.description: Tan or amber Zelon trailing shuck

steps: Mount curved hook. Tie in tan Zelon shuck at bend.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub gray-olive abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 4 open turns.
Tie in gray CDC upright as emerging wing.
Dub olive-gray thorax around wing base, whip finish.

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: Speckled gray CDC or gray poly wing

steps: Mount hook. Tie in speckled gray wing upright, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in split speckled dun hackle tail.
Dub slim gray-olive body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in grizzly hackle, wrap 3-4 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: White or clear poly yarn, spent

steps: Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split speckled tail.
Dub tan-gray body from bend to behind wings.
Dub olive-tan thorax around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Legs
tying_materials.description: Light partridge or wood duck fibers

steps: Mount hook. Lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 wood duck flank fibers as tail.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub pale olive-yellow abdomen to 60%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in light turkey wingcase and light partridge legs.
Dub pale yellow-olive thorax, pull wingcase, whip finish.

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: Pale gray CDC or light poly wing

tying_materials.name: Hackle
tying_materials.description: Pale ginger or cream dry fly hackle

steps: Mount hook. Tie in pale gray wing upright, figure-eight.
Wrap to bend; tie in split cream hackle tail.
Dub pale yellow-olive body from bend to behind wing.
Tie in pale ginger hackle, wrap 4 turns, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.
Mount hook. Figure-eight white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in long split cream tail.
Dub pale yellow body from bend to behind wings.
Dub pale yellow thorax around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Abdomen
tying_materials.description: Pale yellow-cream dubbing, fat burrowing profile

tying_materials.name: Gills
tying_materials.description: Gray ostrich herl tied along sides

tying_materials.name: Wingcase
tying_materials.description: Wide mottled turkey tail, double section

steps: Mount 3XL hook. Lay yellow thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 pheasant tail fibers spread wide as tails.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a very fat pale yellow-cream abdomen to 60% — Hexagenia nymphs are burrowers with a distinctive fat body.
Rib with gold wire in 6 turns.
Tie in ostrich herl gills along each side.
Tie in wide double turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in partridge legs, dub fat pale yellow thorax.
Pull wingcase forward, tie down, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: Moose body hair or dark elk hair, 3 fibers spread

tying_materials.name: Body
tying_materials.description: Pale yellow dubbing with olive-brown segmentation

tying_materials.name: Rib
tying_materials.description: Brown thread or fine wire for segmentation

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: Large gray poly wing or dyed yellow elk hair comparadun

tying_materials.name: Hackle
tying_materials.description: Grizzly or light brown oversized hackle

steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in large comparadun-style gray poly or elk wing.
Wrap to bend; tie in 3 moose body hairs spread as tails.
Dub a fat pale yellow body segmented with brown thread rib, from bend to wing.
Tie in grizzly hackle, wrap 3-4 turns.
Tie off, whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: Moose body hair, 3 long fibers spread

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: White poly yarn, large spent wings

steps: Mount 2XL hook. Figure-eight large white poly spent wings at mid-shank.
Wrap to bend; tie in 3 long moose hair tails, spread.
Dub pale yellow-olive body from bend to behind wings.
Dub olive-brown thorax, figure-eighting around wing bases.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Legs
tying_materials.description: Brown partridge or olive rubber legs

steps: Mount 3XL hook. Lay olive-brown thread base.
Tie in 2 olive-brown goose biots as split tails.
Tie in fine copper wire.
Dub a flat olive-brown abdomen to 60% — flatten with fingers to mimic stonefly profile.
Rib with copper wire in 5 turns.
Tie in wide mottled turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in partridge legs or olive rubber legs.
Dub dark olive-brown thorax, pull wingcase, tie in biot antennae.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: Elk hair short stub or olive elk butts

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: Natural olive-dyed elk hair, low-riding style

steps: Mount 2XL hook. Tie in short elk hair tail stub at bend.
Tie in brown saddle hackle.
Dub olive-brown body to 3/4 of shank.
Palmer hackle in 5 open spirals forward.
Tie in olive or natural elk hair wing flat over body — Skwala adults ride low.
Optional: tie in brown rubber legs on each side.
Trim elk butts, build head, whip finish, head cement.

rec_green_caddis_larva_nymph.hook: Tiemco TMC 2457 scud hook or TMC 3761, sizes 12-16

tying_materials.name: Abdomen
tying_materials.description: Bright green dubbing or green Antron — Rhyacophila are free-living, bright green

tying_materials.name: Head
tying_materials.description: Black or dark brown dubbing ball

steps: Mount scud hook. Lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in fine wire at bend.
Dub a bright green abdomen filling the curved shank to 75%.
Rib with wire in 5-6 turns to segment body.
Dub a darker brown thorax at the front 25%.
Build a small dark dubbing head, whip finish, head cement.
Optional: add copper or gold bead for weight.

tying_materials.name: Wing pads
tying_materials.description: Dark gray hen back or CDC folded flat over thorax

steps: Mount curved hook. Lay olive thread to bend.
Tie in fine gold wire.
Dub a bright green abdomen to 65%.
Rib in 5 turns.
Tie in dark gray hen back flat over thorax area as wing pads.
Dub olive-brown thorax.
Wrap 2 turns of soft partridge hackle, tie off.
Whip finish, head cement.

tying_materials.name: Body
tying_materials.description: Tan or gray foam cylinder or dubbed body, segmented

tying_materials.name: Wing
tying_materials.description: Two clear or light gray poly or CDC wings, angled back

tying_materials.name: Legs
tying_materials.description: Knotted tan rubber legs (6), very long — cranefly legs are distinctive

steps: Mount 2XL hook. Start thread at mid-shank.
Tie in a tan foam strip or dub a segmented tan body covering 2/3 of shank.
Dub a slight thorax hump behind the eye.
Tie in two pale poly or CDC wings angled back 45 degrees, one on each side.
Tie in 3 pairs of knotted tan rubber legs — cranefly legs are very long; leave extra length.
Build a neat head, whip finish, head cement.

rec_cranefly_larva_nymph.hook: Tiemco TMC 5263 3XL or scud hook, sizes 8-12

tying_materials.name: Body
tying_materials.description: Cream or tan Ultra Chenille (micro) or tan dubbing — segmented worm-like profile

tying_materials.name: Rib
tying_materials.description: Tan or cream thread counter-wrapped for segmentation

steps: Mount hook. Lay tan thread base to bend.
Tie in tan Ultra Chenille or dub a cream worm-like body the full shank length.
If using dubbing, counter-wrap with thread to create visible segmentation.
Build a small dark brown head at the eye.
Whip finish, head cement.
Optional: add a small black bead as the head for weight.

tying_materials.name: Abdomen
tying_materials.description: Black dubbing, large round ball at rear

tying_materials.name: Thorax
tying_materials.description: Black dubbing, smaller ball at front

tying_materials.name: Wings
tying_materials.description: White or clear poly yarn or CDC, figure-eight spent flat

tying_materials.name: Legs
tying_materials.description: Black hackle 1-2 turns or omit for simplicity

steps: Mount hook. At the rear 2/3, dub a fat round black ball abdomen.
Pinch thread tight to create a distinct narrow waist.
At mid-shank, tie in white poly yarn or CDC and figure-eight to form flat spent wings.
Dub a smaller black ball thorax at the front 1/3.
Optional: wrap 1-2 turns of black hackle at the waist between the body segments.
Whip finish, apply UV resin to the bodies for durability and shine.

tying_materials.name: Tail
tying_materials.description: 3 olive marabou fibers or olive Zelon — mimics caudal gills

tying_materials.name: Abdomen
tying_materials.description: Olive Antron or dubbing, slim tapered

tying_materials.name: Thorax
tying_materials.description: Olive dubbing, slightly fatter than abdomen

steps: Mount 3XL hook. Lay olive thread base to bend.
Tie in 3 olive marabou fibers as tail/caudal gills, splayed slightly.
Tie in fine copper wire.
Dub a slim tapered olive abdomen to 60% — damsel nymphs have a very slim body.
Rib in 6 turns.
Tie in olive turkey tail wingcase.
Tie in olive partridge legs on each side.
Dub a slightly fuller olive thorax, pull wingcase over.
Optional: attach small black mono eyes at the head.
Whip finish, head cement.
